title: MangaShinji vs RebuildShinji
wikitext:
[]: Manga!Shinji vs Rebuild!Shinji             Quote Post by OneShotPunch » Fri Oct 11, 2024 10:25 pm Most people seem to agree that the original anime Shinji is the best written version, so I thought of maybe comparing the other two available. They have some common ground. Both are "better adjusted" versions of the original, share a closer relationship with Rei and Kaworu, help out their versions of Asuka who turn out to be an artificial being, Gendo is more of an ass, and the endings of both have the world being changed to remove Evas/Angels. Who's journey do you prefer? What interesting insights do you get out of this comparison?

[]: Re: Manga!Shinji vs Rebuild!Shinji             Quote Post by dzzthink » Sat Oct 19, 2024 8:10 am I would say that the manga Shinji is quite distinct from the anime Shinji. He seems to clap back at others more often, especially in the case of his relationship with Kaworu, who he very quickly dismisses or insults, so I don't think their relationship is good at all. In comparison, the anime Shinji is rather gentle and demure, rarely even displaying hostility unless provoked excessively, such as during the Angel missions. I definitely agree with the fact that the manga and rebuild endings were similar to some degree. I honestly believed that the manga Shinji was capable of succeeding in the future, as he displayed a great deal of courage in the instrumentality, whereas the change in rebuild was rather sudden. I guess the manga progression was more realistic but I still prefer the anime Shinji, who is more gentle and sympathetic.

[]: Re: Manga!Shinji vs Rebuild!Shinji             Quote Post by BernardoCairo » Sat Nov 02, 2024 11:18 am From my point of view, Manga Shinji is probably the most shallow of the three versions of the character. However, he is also probably the coolest in terms of behavior. He is a hero. He actually saved Asuka at the end, for example. And he's definitely more assertive. I mean, Shinji actually confronts Kaworu in the manga. And Misato too. But ultimately, I think he misses a bit of what makes Shinji a unique character. But if you look past that and see the manga for what it is, I think he's fine. I don't like his struggle though. "I can't save anyone." Way to superficial and lame. And he has a fixation on Rei that goes too far. NTE Shinji on the other hand is more like what you would expect from Shinji as a character. He turns out to be a hero in the end. But that's the result of his growth as a character, not what he is from the beginning. I agree that the transition from the lowest point he had ever been at to that happened very quickly. And without much context too. But I can see what they were going for. To be honest, my favorite version of Shinji within NTE is his Q self, when he acts like a crying baby/confused boy. I simply like him having a mental breakdown and having to deal with it right after his heroic moment at the end of the second movie. At the end of the day, OG Shinji from NGE/EOE is the best one anyways.

[]: Re: Manga!Shinji vs Rebuild!Shinji             Quote Post by OneShotPunch » Sun Nov 03, 2024 9:18 am BernardoCairo wrote: NTE Shinji on the other hand is more like what you would expect from Shinji as a character. He turns out to be a hero in the end. But that's the result of his growth as a character, not what he is from the beginning.

[]: Re: Manga!Shinji vs Rebuild!Shinji             Quote Post by BernardoCairo » Sun Nov 03, 2024 9:41 am He's a more active character, sure. We see that at the end of HA as well. But he doesn't strike me as a "hero" regardless. At least not until the second half of the last movie. You can see in the first two films, and especially the third, that he was working mostly for himself. He put his needs before others'. And I'm not saying he was wrong to do that, but it's very clear that this changed in Shin. He put the needs of his friends first and was ready to sacrifice himself for humanity in the end. All to say, this version of Shinji has some "poison" in him. And while that's not necessarily a bad thing, it changes over the course of the story.
